# docusaurus-plugin-llms: Issues and Fixes Report

_Last updated: April 22, 2026_

---

## Overview

After integrating `docusaurus-plugin-llms` into the Mobiscroll docs build, three categories of errors appeared during `npm run build`. This document lists each issue, its root cause, and the fix applied.

---

## Issue 1 — Backslash in `_auto-generated` partial import paths

### Error

```
[docusaurus-plugin-llms] Failed to resolve partial import from ../_auto-generated/eventcalendar/options_agenda.md:
ENOENT: no such file or directory, open '.../docs/angular/\_auto-generated/eventcalendar/options_agenda.md'
```

### Root cause

Some import statements in the docs used an escaped underscore (`\_`) in the path:

```mdx
import Options from '../\_auto-generated/eventcalendar/options_agenda.md';
```

The backslash is a **literal character** (ASCII `0x5C`) in the raw file content — it is NOT a Markdown escape here because MDX import paths are string literals, not prose text. When `docusaurus-plugin-llms` reads the file and extracts the import path via regex, it captures the raw string including the backslash.

On macOS/Linux (POSIX), Node.js `path.resolve()` does **not** treat `\` as a path separator; it is a valid filename character. So `path.resolve('.../angular/eventcalendar', '../\_auto-generated/...')` resolves to `.../angular/\_auto-generated/...` — a path with a literal backslash in the directory name — which does not exist on disk. The actual directory is `_auto-generated` (no backslash).

Note: this problem is macOS/Linux-specific. On Windows, `path.resolve` treats `\` as a path separator, so it would have worked there by coincidence.

### Affected files

349 import statements across **100 files** — the same set of 20 doc pages (eventcalendar, datepicker, select, forms, notifications components) repeated across all 5 frameworks: `angular`, `react`, `vue`, `javascript`, `jquery`.

### Fix

Removed the backslash from all affected import paths using a Node.js script that read each file, replaced every occurrence of the literal byte `0x5C` immediately before `_auto-generated`, and wrote the file back.

```
# Before
import Options from '../\_auto-generated/eventcalendar/options_agenda.md';

# After
import Options from '../_auto-generated/eventcalendar/options_agenda.md';
```

---

## Issue 2 — Backslash in `_shared` partial import paths

### Error

```
[docusaurus-plugin-llms] Failed to resolve partial import from ../../\_shared/getting-started/supported_platforms.mdx:
ENOENT: no such file or directory, open '.../docs/\_shared/getting-started/supported_platforms.mdx'
```

### Root cause

Identical to Issue 1 — a literal backslash before `_shared` in import paths:

```mdx
import SupportedPlatforms from '../../\_shared/getting-started/supported_platforms.mdx';
```

The actual directory on disk is `_shared` (no backslash), but `path.resolve` on POSIX resolves the path to `\_shared`, which doesn't exist.

### Affected files

40 import statements across **15 files** — getting-started and eventcalendar pages across multiple frameworks.

### Fix

Same approach as Issue 1: replaced all literal `0x5C` characters immediately before `_shared` in import paths.

```
# Before
import SupportedPlatforms from '../../\_shared/getting-started/supported_platforms.mdx';

# After
import SupportedPlatforms from '../../_shared/getting-started/supported_platforms.mdx';
```

---

## Issue 3 — Broken links to generated `.txt` files

### Error

```
[WARNING] Docusaurus found broken links!

- Broken link on source page path = /docs/llms-content:
   -> linking to /docs/llms-javascript.txt
   -> linking to /docs/llms-react.txt
   ...
```

### Root cause

`docs/llms-content.md` and `docs/llms-content-full.md` linked to the plugin-generated `.txt` files using root-absolute paths:

```md
[Mobiscroll for JavaScript](/llms-javascript.txt)
```

The site is configured with `baseUrl: '/docs'`. Docusaurus's built-in link checker resolves root-absolute links (starting with `/`) by prepending the `baseUrl`, turning `/llms-javascript.txt` into `/docs/llms-javascript.txt`. The `.txt` files are generated by the plugin directly into the `build/` root (at `/llms-javascript.txt`), so they are not Docusaurus-managed pages and are not known to the link checker — hence the broken link warning.

### Fix

Deleted `llms-content.md` and `llms-content-full.md` from `versioned_docs/version-6.0.0/`. Nothing in the site links to these pages, so removing them creates no new broken links.

The LLM plugin reads these files directly from `docs/` (the unversioned source tree, scanned via `docsDir: 'docs'`). It does not read from `versioned_docs/` at all, so the files in `docs/` are unaffected and the plugin continues to work.

**Why `exclude` in `docusaurus.config.js` didn't work:** the docs plugin's `exclude` option applies only to the current (unversioned) docs directory. Because `includeCurrentVersion: false` is set, the current version is never built. The pages at `/docs/llms-content` were rendered from `versioned_docs/version-6.0.0/` — the only path the exclude never touched.

**Why `pathname://` didn't work:** `pathname://` is handled only by the client-side `Link` React component (to opt out of SPA navigation). The build-time broken link checker is separate: it strips `pathname://`, then `maybeAddBaseUrl()` prepends the `baseUrl` (`/docs`), giving `/docs/llms-javascript.txt`, which is still not a known route — so the warning persisted regardless.

**Affected files:** `versioned_docs/version-6.0.0/llms-content.md`, `versioned_docs/version-6.0.0/llms-content-full.md` (deleted).

---

## Summary

| # | Issue | Files affected | Imports / links fixed |
|---|-------|---------------|----------------------|
| 1 | Backslash in `_auto-generated` import paths | 100 | 349 |
| 2 | Backslash in `_shared` import paths | 15 | 40 |
| 3 | Broken links to plugin-generated `.txt` files | 2 | 17 |

### Why the backslashes existed

The escaped underscores (`\_`) are a Markdown convention to prevent `_word_` from being interpreted as emphasis. This is valid and harmless in prose, but incorrect inside MDX import path strings, where the path is a JavaScript string literal and backslash has no special meaning as a Markdown escape — it is stored as a literal `\` byte and passed directly to `path.resolve`.
